name: Indian Mackerel
description: A common catch in the region’s coastal markets.

name: Milkfish
description: The most important cultured fish in the Philippines, widely farmed in brackish water ponds and coastal areas of Iloilo and Capiz.

name: Pufferfish
description: Though consumed with caution, locals in Antique are known to prepare specific non-toxic species.

name: Mullet
description: This mild-flavored fish thrives in river mouths and brackish waters in Western Visayas, especially in Iloilo River.

name: Flying Fish
description: Known for its ability to glide above the water's surface, it's occasionally caught in Sulu Sea waters off southern Antique.

name: Rabbitfish
description: A popular breakfast fish, often dried and served with vinegar. Common in the shallow waters around Guimaras and Negros Occidental.

name: Cod
description: Not traditionally native but now locally caught and served in select areas of Bohol and southern Panay, such as Iloilo.

name: Grouper
description: A prized reef fish found in marine protected areas and coral reefs, especially off the coasts of Iloilo and Negros.

name: Red Snapper
description: A high-value fish known for its white flesh. Found in deep waters and reef systems off Antique and Capiz.

name: Trevally
description: Also called Jackfish, it is a strong game fish commonly caught off the coast of Iloilo and Antique.

name: Sardine
description: Caught seasonally in large numbers along the coasts of Aklan and Antique. Frequently used in canning and drying.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A fast-swimming predator highly sought after for its flavorful meat. Found in the open waters of the Visayan Sea.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: A migratory pelagic fish commonly caught in local fisheries. Known for its speed and firm, flavorful meat.

name: Milkfish
description: The most important cultured fish in the country; raised in brackish ponds.

name: Rabbitfish
description: Herbivorous reef fish often dried and fried; a breakfast staple.

name: Grouper
description: Large reef predators popular in markets and restaurants.

name: Red Snapper
description: A prized catch with white flesh; often grilled or steamed.

name: Mackerel Tuna
description: Fast pelagic fish known for firm, flavorful meat.

name: Indian Mackerel
description: Fast pelagic fish known for firm, flavorful meat.

name: Sardine
description: Small schooling fish dried, canned, or fermented.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: Fast-swimming game fish sought for its rich meat.

name: Trevally
description: Strong game fish found in both reefs and open waters.

name: Flying Fish
description: Capable of gliding above water; caught near reef edges.

name: Cod
description: Introduced species found in deeper waters; firm white meat

name: Mullet
description: Bottom-feeding fish found in estuaries and river mouths.

name: Pufferfish
description: Inflates when threatened; some species are toxic.

name: Seahorse
description: Camouflaged fish that swims upright; lives among seagrasses.

name: Blue-Spotted Stingray
description: Small stingray with blue spots; hides on sandy seafloor.

name: Blue Crab
description: Swimming crab with blue-tinted claws; valued for its meat.

name: Mud Crab
description: Brackish-water crab with large claws; farmed and wild-caught.

name: Long-Spined Urchin
description: Black sea urchin with venomous spines; grazes on algae.

name: Sea Cucumber
description: Soft-bodied echinoderm used in soups and exported dried.

name: Sea Grape
description: Edible green seaweed eaten fresh in salads.

name: Tiger Prawn
description: Large farmed prawn, especially in mangrove estuaries.

name: Octopus
description: Highly intelligent mollusk; uses camouflage and jet propulsion.

name: Moray Eel
description: Long, sharp-toothed eel often found hiding in reef crevices.

name: Squid
description: Cephalopod with tentacles and ink defense; grilled or dried.

name: Scad
description: Staple food fish; sold fresh or smoked.

name: Anchovy
description: Tiny fish used in dried snacks or fermented paste

name: Parrotfish
description: Coral-eating fish with beak-like teeth; often brightly colored.

name: Batfish
description: Disc-shaped reef fish; swims near coral drop-offs.

name: Sailfin Snapper
description: Deep-bodied reef fish with a distinctive dorsal fin.

name: Triton Trumpet
description: Large predatory snail that feeds on crown-of-thorns starfish.
